page: Amazon Web Services c6gn.large is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the ARMv8 architecture, it features 2 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Neoverse-N1 model running at 2.5 GHz. The server is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage options. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 3 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate moderate single-core capabilities alongside lower multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics. The stress-ng single-core CPU test yielded an average score of 1515.29, while the multi-core score of 3014.06 falls into the lower performance tier. Memory bandwidth tests showed an average read-only large block speed of 38.34 GB/sec, but a weaker write speed of 18.72 GB/sec. Other benchmarks, such as Geekbench (961.0 single-core, 1749.0 multi-core) and Passmark CPU (1344.13), reflect entry-level compute performance for ARM-based workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c6gn.large offers a low-cost entry point into AWS's custom ARM64 ecosystem, providing dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading. However, resource-intensive tasks like software compilation, database operations, and LLM inference show poor performance relative to larger instances. This server is best suited for lightweight web serving, basic network-optimized tasks, and testing or development of ARM64-compatible applications.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.large is a compute-optimized, ARM64-based server powered by a 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores, 4.0 GB of system memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps, running on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Benchmark results show average single-core CPU performance (stress-ng score of 1515.29) and average large-block read memory bandwidth (38.34 GB/sec), though multi-core and write-heavy operations fall into lower performance tiers. This server provides a cost-efficient, entry-level option for ARM64 development, lightweight web serving, and basic network-optimized workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.large is an ARM64 compute-optimized server with 2 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-N1 cores, 4 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth, suitable for lightweight ARM64 workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.large is a compute-optimized ARM64 server featuring 2 Graviton cores, 4 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An entry-level, ARM64-based compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores and baseline network performance for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor with 2 dedicated cores
Equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory and 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average single-core CPU performance and large-block memory read speeds
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for lightweight web serving, network-optimized tasks, and ARM64 development
